title: Embedding the derived category of a curve into a Fano variety
speaker: Alexander Kuznetsov
abstract: According to the conjecture of Bondal, the derived category of coherent sheaves on any smooth projective variety can be embedded as a semiorthogonal component into the derived category of a Fano variety of higher dimension. I will explain how this embedding can be constructed for general curves of arbitrary genus. This is a joint work with Anton Fonarev.

proof_stages.label: Derived categories, semiorthogonal decompositions, and Fano precedents
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes the meaning of D(X), recalls semiorthogonal decompositions of triangulated categories, and reviews known Fano examples where residual components can be derived categories of curves.

proof_stages.label: Bondal conjecture and Fourier-Mukai full-faithfulness test
proof_stages.summary: Bondal's conjecture is stated and specialized to curves, while Orlov representability and the Bondal-Orlov criterion reduce the desired embedding to constructing a Fourier-Mukai kernel whose fiberwise Ext groups match those of points on the curve.

proof_stages.label: Rank-two fixed-determinant moduli as the Fano candidate
proof_stages.summary: For a curve C the proposed Fano variety is the moduli space SU_C(2,L) of rank-two bundles with fixed odd determinant, equipped with its universal bundle, leaving the problem of computing Exts between the universal-bundle restrictions.

proof_stages.label: Hyperelliptic Desale-Ramanan model and theorem
proof_stages.summary: For hyperelliptic C the relevant moduli spaces are identified with varieties M_k of k-planes in the intersection of two quadrics, and the main hyperelliptic theorem asserts that natural functors D(C)->D(M_k) are fully faithful for k≤g−1, including the fixed-determinant moduli case.

proof_stages.label: Spinor-bundle construction of the hyperelliptic kernels
proof_stages.summary: The functors Phi_k are constructed by taking spinor bundles on orthogonal Grassmannians in the pencil of quadrics, including the degenerate fibers, and showing that these assemble into a vector bundle S_k on C×M_k that is universal when k=g−1.

proof_stages.label: Ext computation by base change and spinor resolutions
proof_stages.summary: The Bondal-Orlov Ext criterion is verified by expressing M_k as a Tor-independent Cartesian intersection of orthogonal Grassmannians, reducing Exts to cohomology on the ordinary Grassmannian, and applying an explicit symmetric-Young-diagram resolution plus Borel-Weil-Bott computations.

proof_stages.label: Openness deformation to general curves
proof_stages.summary: In a family of curves with odd relative determinant, full faithfulness of the relative Fourier-Mukai functor is shown to be an open condition via the cone of the canonical map from the adjoint-composition kernel to O_Δ, so the hyperelliptic result implies Bondal's conjecture for a general curve.

examples.name: Projective space full exceptional collection
examples.note: The Beilinson decomposition of D(P^n) into line bundles is used as the simplest concrete illustration of a semiorthogonal decomposition, not as a step in the proof.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker states Beilinson’s semiorthogonal decomposition of projective space by line bundles but does not verify exceptionality or compute anything on it

examples.name: Fano threefolds of degrees 18, 16, and 12
examples.note: These Fano threefolds are cited as known cases where the residual semiorthogonal component is the derived category of a curve of genus 2, 3, or 7, motivating the general conjectural picture.
examples.engagement_reason: the degree 18, 16, and 12 Fano threefolds are listed with their semiorthogonal decompositions and curve genera, but no construction or calculation is carried out on them

examples.name: Codimension-three linear section of Gr(3,6)
examples.note: The Grassmannian linear section with an elliptic-curve residual category is another motivating homological-projective-duality example of curves inside Fano derived categories.
